nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I lean more towards collaborating. I like to have the final look at everything before I send it out, so I use it more as a partner than a subordinate

nodes.label: AI for document analysis and proposal development
nodes.grounding_span: I'll take the RFP or RFQ and throw it into ChatGPT, and then I have a voice conversation with the AI while I'm commuting to the office to help me understand the RFP

nodes.label: AI for administrative and writing tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I've used it to do simple things like take my thoughts on one of my subordinates and convert that into a write up for either an award or for an annual performance review

nodes.label: AI for quality assurance and error detection
nodes.grounding_span: I put it into an AI and it found a ton of things that I didn't catch, from inconsistencies with earlier sections in the proposal, to misleading sentences or sentences that didn't make sense

nodes.label: AI for sensitive or personal tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI for sensitive or personal tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle myself is the personal aspects of managing employees... I also tend to do the financial tasks on my own, just so any sensitive info remains on company servers/computers

nodes.label: AI reliability and memory
nodes.grounding_span: when I first started my RFP review process (prior to having voice conversations), it would forget what I was doing or act like it didn't have access to the files that I sent

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: Doing it this way saved me a good 1-2 hours, so it was nice

nodes.label: Quality and thoroughness
nodes.grounding_span: it found a ton of things that I didn't catch

nodes.label: Human judgment and personal touch
nodes.grounding_span: I know my employees, so I want to provide all of the input and just have it shape it into a nicer sounding format

nodes.label: Security and confidentiality
nodes.grounding_span: just so any sensitive info remains on company servers/computers

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I lean more towards collaborating. I like to have the final look at everything before I send it out, so I use it more as a partner than a subordinate

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for document analysis
nodes.grounding_span: it kept up with me, and remembered exactly what I was looking for

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for error detection
nodes.grounding_span: it found a ton of things that I didn't catch... saved me a good 1-2 hours, so it was nice

nodes.label: Frustration with early AI forgetfulness
nodes.grounding_span: when I first started my RFP review process... it would forget what I was doing or act like it didn't have access to the files that I sent

nodes.label: Preference for human handling of sensitive tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle myself is the personal aspects of managing employees... I also tend to do the financial tasks on my own

nodes.label: Systematic step-by-step decomposition of complex documents
nodes.grounding_span: I first ask it to give me an overview of the opportunity... Then I'll ask it what it thinks is the key focus... Then I'll go into the Section L and M... After the initial reviews, I'll go more into depth

nodes.label: Proactive adoption and advocacy for AI tools
nodes.grounding_span: I've adopted using AI at my company in the ways that I do earlier than all of my colleagues... I share what I do with my team, as well as my peers in the company to show them how much easier things are
